cards.name: 18 1/2-Minute Gap
attributes: Requires Discards and All Illuminati Actions

cards.name: Adepts of Hermes
cards.text: If you fail an Attack to Control against a Group from our own hand, you do no tlose the group . . . Just return the card to your hand. The Adepts of Hermes have +6 on any attempt ot control or destroy a Magic group. SPECIAL GOAL: Each Magic resource you control counts as one group toward the Basic Goal.

cards.name: Advanced Supersonic Aluminum Nazi Hell Creatures from Beneath the Hollow Earth
American Civil Liberties Union
cards.text: German Version: Autonome

cards.name: An Offer You Can't Refuse
attributes: Draw no Group Cards This Turn

cards.name: Angela Merkel
cards.text: German Version: Helmut Kohl

cards.name: Anything Worth Doing Is Worth Overdoing
Atlantis
cards.text: During your turn, you may expose 2 randomly selected plots from the hand of a rival. This is a free move. SPECIAL GOAL: Control groups with total global power of 35 or more, including Atlantis' own global power, reduces your basic goal by 3. This goal may not be combined with any other goal.

cards.name: Autonome
cards.text: Gives +10 on any attempt to destroy any Government group. The Autonome are anarchists. Most are actually peaceful, but the violent ones get the news coverage.

cards.name: Bank Merger
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Bavarian Illuminati
cards.text: Each turn, you may declare one of your attackes privileged. SPECIAL GOAL: Control a total Power of 50 or more, counting Bavaria's own Power.

cards.name: Bermuda Triangle
cards.text: You may reorganize your groups freely at the end of your turn. SPECIAL GOAL: Control a total Power of at least 35, counting Bermuda's own Power, and at leas one group of each alignment. A group with more than one alignment counts for all its alignments.

cards.name: Bernie Madoff
cards.text: German Version: Dagobert

cards.name: Burschenschaften
cards.text: You may spend this group's action to draw another Group card at any time. The Burschenschaften count as two conservative groups towards any goal. These are conservative, all-male student groups which affect military dress.

cards.name: Charismatic Leader
cards.headline: Linked to Donald Trump
cards.context: Depicts a blonde demagogue rallying supporters. Drawn in 1994 as a generic political archetype, years before Trump's presidency — when he was widely seen as a bankrupt casino owner, not a future head of state.

cards.name: China
cards.text: Explicitly gets its +20 defense against Disasters.

cards.name: Chris Angel
cards.text: German Version: Der grosse Magier

cards.name: Church of the SubGenius 1
cards.text: The Church of the SubGenius may accumulate its Action tokens (Slack) each turn, though it can spend only one per action. It, and any SubGenius groups in its Power Structure, has +2 for direct control of any SubGenius group. SPECIAL GOAL: Up to three Slack (Illuminati) tokens on the Church of the SubGenius may count as groups toward your Basic Goal. This Goal cannot be combined with any other Goal.

cards.name: Church of the SubGenius 2
cards.text: The Church of the SubGenius may accumulate its Action tokens (Slack) each turn, though it can spend only one per action. It, and any SubGenius groups in its Power Structure, has +2 for direct control of any SubGenius group. SPECIAL GOAL: Up to three Slack (Illuminati) tokens on the Church of the SubGenius may count as groups toward your Basic Goal. This Goal cannot be combined with any other Goal.

cards.name: Church of the SubGenius 3
cards.text: The Church of the SubGenius may accumulate its Action tokens (Slack) each turn, though it can spend only one per action. It, and any SubGenius groups in its Power Structure, has +2 for direct control of any SubGenius group. SPECIAL GOAL: Up to three Slack (Illuminati) tokens on the Church of the SubGenius may count as groups toward your Basic Goal. This Goal cannot be combined with any other Goal.

cards.name: Church of the SubGenius 4
cards.text: The Church of the SubGenius may accumulate its Action tokens (Slack) each turn, though it can spend only one per action. It, and any SubGenius groups in its Power Structure, has +2 for direct control of any SubGenius group. SPECIAL GOAL: Up to three Slack (Illuminati) tokens on the Church of the SubGenius may count as groups toward your Basic Goal. This Goal cannot be combined with any other Goal.

cards.name: Clipper Chip
cards.text: Limited to one per player.

cards.name: Combined Disasters
cards.text: Requires that both Disasters must be eligible to strike the target Place.

cards.name: CONspiracy
cards.text: Gives +1 Power to all your other Weird groups. The German INWO/Magic convention, which hosts the German national M:TG championship.

cards.name: Corporate Ownership of the Public Airwaves
Couch Potatoes
cards.text: German Version: Manta-Fahrer

cards.name: Cover-Up
attributes: May Require Secret or Illuminati Action

cards.name: D.E.A.
cards.text: German Version: Hipos

cards.name: Dagobert
cards.text: Once per turn, Dagobert can try, as a free action, to steal one Action token from any Corporate group. Roll a die. 1-3 means bad luck; nothing happens. 4-6 means success. Dagobert gets the stolen token, but can only use it for his own defense. Dagobert may only steal tokens before they are used . . . he may not wait until the token is used and try to steal it them! A famous German criminal, who specialized in bomb-threat extortion schemes. He's now in jail. (In German, "Uncle Scrooge" is called Dagobert Duck.)

cards.name: David Letterman
cards.text: German Version: Talkmaster

cards.name: Der grosse Magier
cards.text: Gives +2 on any attempt to control any Magic group. Has +10 for direct control of any Magic group. The Great Magician - a stage magician.

cards.name: Discordian Society
cards.text: You have +4 on any attempt to control Weird groups. Your power structure is immune to attacks from Government or Straight groups, and to all special abilities of these groups. SPECIAL GOAL: Any Weird group with a Power of 3 or more counts double toward your total number of groups controlled.

cards.name: Dollars for Decency
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Don't Forget To Smash The State
Eliza
cards.text: Limited to one per Group.

cards.name: Enough is Enough
attributes: Draw No Plot Cards This Turn

cards.name: Epidemic
cards.headline: Linked to Covid-19
cards.context: A standard Disaster card showing plague imagery. Pandemics are perennial conspiracy imagery; this card predates Covid-19 by over two decades as part of INWO's dystopian satire.

cards.name: Erich Honecker
cards.text: You may place up to three Plots under Erich. They don't count against your limit, and cannot be exposed. You can use any of them at any time. Before using one, you must remove it from under Erich and roll a die. On a 1 or 3, discard it! On a 4 through 6, use it, paying the normal actions or other costs. Honecker was the last leader of East Germany, before reunification. His card replaces Fidel Castro in the German set, but his ability is different.

cards.name: Eternal Salvation or Triple your Money Back
Evil Geniuses for a Better Tomorrow
Ex-Hippies
cards.text: German Version: Karnevalisten

cards.name: Fasces Romanum
cards.text: You start your turn by drawing two group cards, rather than one. SPECIAL GOAL: Any group counts double towards your total number of groups controlled, as long as none of your rivals control a group with higher permanent resistance.

cards.name: Flower Power
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Freaking The Mundanes
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Friend Computer
cards.text: When you make an attack, the modifier for shared/opposed alignments is increased by +2/-2. SPECIAL GOAL: Each successful attack to control or destroy by another rplayer, made with your aid, reduces your basic goal by 1. This goal may not be combined with other goals in any way.

cards.name: Full Moon
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Fussballprofis
cards.text: The Fussballprofis' Corporate puppets add the attribute Media. Pro Soccer Players. This group replaces Professional Sports, but with different abilities. Soccer is the leading sport in Germany, and the German national team is one of the best in the world.

cards.name: Gang War
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Geek Conventions
cards.text: German Version: CONspiracy

cards.name: Genschman
cards.text: Genschman is liberal when he feels like it. He is treated as Liberal if and only if you, the Illuminati pulling his strings, want him to be Liberal. He gets a +2 bonus any time he aids in the defense of any Nation. Hans-Dietrich Genscher, former Foreign Minister of Germany, who still has considerable influence throughout Europe.

cards.name: George Bush
cards.headline: 1990s politics
cards.context: The sitting president at the game's launch, included alongside other world leaders and celebrities of the era.

cards.name: Giselle Bandchen
cards.text: German Version: Topmodell

cards.name: Give Me Slack, Or Give Me Food
Gnomes of Zurich
cards.text: You may hold 6 Plot cards in your hand, rather than the usual 5. You have +4 on any attempt to control any Bank. SPECIAL GOAL: Any Corporate group or Bank with a Power 4 or more counts double toward your total number of groups controlled. Have a +4 bonus to control Bank Groups only (not Bank and Corporate Groups).

cards.name: Good Sex for Mutants Dating League
Hannelore Kohl
cards.text: The Red Cross gets an extra Action token every turn if Hannelore is in the same Power Structure. The wife of Chancellor Helmut Kohl. Very active in the campaign against cancer.

cards.name: Helmut Kohl
cards.text: Helmut gives a +3 on any attempt to control any Government group that is part of Germany. He has a +8 for direct control of any Government group. Any time Helmut's alignment matters, roll a die. On a 1-3, he's Straight at the moment. On a 4-6, he's not. Chancellor of Germany since 1982, and leader of the Christdemokraten. Cartoonists love to caricature him with a pear-shaped head.

cards.name: Hillary Clinton
cards.headline: 1990s politics
cards.context: Features the then-First Lady as a Personality card. Contemporary satire of 1990s power players, not a prediction of later events.

cards.name: Hipos
cards.text: Spend this group's action to choose one player. Your victim must remove one Action token from each of his groups that has more than one. Parking-meter readers - not police, but a volunteer auxiliary. They are not beloved . . .

cards.name: It's Time to Bring Out the Secret Weapon
Jiang Hu
cards.text: Any attack roll of 11 you make is not an automatic failure. You may exchange plot draws for group draws and vice versa. SPECIAL GOAL: Control and/or destroy criminal groups with total power of 40 or more. This reduces your basic goal by 4. Destroyed groups contribute the permanent power they had at the time of destruction.

cards.name: Jimmy Carter
cards.text: German Version: Genschman

cards.name: Joschka Fischer
cards.text: Has a +4 for direct control of any Green group. By using his action, Joschka can cancel an action of the Nuclear Power Companies. Leader of the German Green party, anti-nuclear activist, never seen without tennis shoes.

cards.name: Karnevalisten
cards.text: Gives +2 on any attempt to control any Weird group. During other players' turn, the Karnevalisten are Straight. During your own turn, they are Weird. (In a two-player game, they're Weird only on every second turn.) Carnival-goers. Think of Mardi Gras . . . Every February, a lot of seemingly-normal Germans get very weird, and keep it up until the beer is gone.

cards.name: Kin Jong Il
cards.text: German Version: Erich Honecker

cards.name: Kronopians
cards.text: You may spend the Kronopians action to pick a plot card from yoru discard pile and put it in your hand. If you also discard a group card, the chosen plot card may be from the discard pile of a rival. SPECIAL GOAL: After your second turn, you may reduce your basic goal by one at the beginning of each of your turns. This goal may not be combined with other goals in any way.

cards.name: Laura Bush
cards.text: German Version: Hannelore Kohl

cards.name: Manta-Fahrer
cards.text: They're so stupid, they believe everything in the commercials! By spending one Illuminati action, or action(s) of Media groups with total power of 6 or more, you may add any one alignment to the Manta-Fahrer. This effect lasts until the beginning of your next turn. You can't add an alignment that is opposite to one they already have. Manta drivers - a Manta is a sports car. The Germans tell "Manta driver" jokes as well as blonde jokes. Destroying the Manta drivers will always draw cheers in a German game. The traditional Manta driver dates a hairdresser - thus the scissors.

cards.name: March On Washington
cards.headline: Linked to the Capitol riot
cards.context: Shows mass protest at the seat of government. Cited in hindsight after January 6, 2021, but reflects common 1990s political-satire tropes about civil unrest.

cards.name: Market Manipulation
cards.headline: Financial conspiracy
cards.context: Depicts deliberate Wall Street manipulation — a staple of conspiracy fiction and a core INWO theme of shadowy power brokers.

cards.name: Martial Law
cards.headline: Linked to civil unrest
cards.context: Government crackdown imagery often compared to barbed-wire scenes near the White House during 2020 protests. A generic dystopian trope, not a specific forecast.

cards.name: Military Massacre
attributes: Requires All Illuminati Actions or Discards

cards.name: My Karma Ran Over Your Dogma
Neoconservaaties
cards.text: German Version: Burschenschaften

cards.name: New Federal Budget
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: NGOs
cards.text: German Version: Zuvieldienstleistende

cards.name: Orbit One
cards.text: Can be affected by a Nuclear Disaster, but not an Earthquake.

cards.name: Philosophers
cards.text: German Version: Stammtischpolitiker

cards.name: Plague of Demons
cards.headline: Chaos in Washington
cards.context: Supernatural apocalypse descending on DC. Part of INWO's tongue-in-cheek conspiracy aesthetic rather than a literal prediction.

cards.name: Pledge Drive
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Political Correctness
cards.text: Affects Conservative Groups with a Power of 0 or 1.
cards.headline: Culture-war satire
cards.context: Shows hanged figures with absurd 'crime' signs like 'Used Insensitive Pronoun.' Intended as parody of political-correctness panic; later circulated in culture-war media.

cards.name: Pro Footballers
cards.text: German Version: Fussballprofis

cards.name: Red Scare
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Reload!
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power’s worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Rush Limbaugh
cards.text: German Version: Showmaster

cards.name: Science Cannot Remove the Terror of the Gods!
Secrets Man Was Not Meant To Know
attributes: Requires Discards or All Illuminati Actions

cards.name: Seize The Time!
cards.text: Requires an Illuminati action, and cannot be used on your first turn. It does not give the Illuminati any new Action tokens, and you cannot draw cards or play any Plots during your extra turn.

cards.name: Servants of Cthulhu
cards.text: You have +4 on any attempt to destroy, even Disasters and Assassinations. Draw a Plot card henever you destroy a group! SPECIAL GOAL: For every group you destroy, reduce by 1 the number of groups you need to control in order to win. You may also count Illuminati which you destroy by removing their last group. If you destroy 8 groups, you win, regardless of how many you control!

cards.name: Shangri-La
cards.text: Any group in your Power Structure has an extra +5 to defend against any attack, even Instants. You cannot destroy any groups except Violent ones and rival Illuminati. SPECIAL GOAL: Have Peaceful groups with a total Power of 30 in play, regardless of who controls them! If this happens, all Shangri-La players share the victory. Explicitly gets its +5 to defend against Instant Attacks.

cards.name: Showmaster
cards.text: Going on his show is always a gamble . . .  Spend his Action token to roll one die for any other Personality, with the consent of that Personality's owner. On a 1-3, the Personality's printed power is reduced permanently to 1. On a 4-6, it is permanently doubled. Only one appearance is allowed for each Personality. A talk show host (replaces Gordo Remora, with different abilities)

cards.name: Society of Assassins
cards.text: When one of your Fanatic groups attacks or defends, you may treat its Fanatic alignment as the same as that of any other Fanatic group. Your Fanatic groups also have Global Power equal to their Power. SPECIAL GOAL: Any Secret group counts double toward your total number of groups controlled, as long as none of your rivals control a Secret group with more power.

cards.name: Stammtischpolitiker
cards.text: Constant discussion leads to illumination and the truth... The Stammtischpolitiker can use their action to reverse any alignment of any group in play. This effect lasts until your next turn. Political discussion (and drinking) clubs, mainly of old men.

cards.name: Stepp Blatter
cards.text: German Version: Supertrainer

cards.name: Steven Chu
cards.text: German Version: Joschka Fischer

cards.name: Supertrainer
cards.text: By using his action, the Supertrainer can put a just-played World Cup Victory into your hand. He can do that only once per turn. He has +10 for direct control of the Fussballprofis. The Soccer Coach from Hell. In Germany, a good coach sits at the right hand of God . . .

cards.name: Talkmaster
cards.text: I'm a very good cook. And a very good butler too . . .  He gets +6 for direct control of any Personality. Another talk show host; in Germany, a "talkmaster" hosts a more serious program, while a "showmaster" is small talk and media flash.

cards.name: Tax Breaks
cards.text: Now require an Illuminati action and reload only 5 Power's worth of tokens, or any one Group of any Power. The rules now make it explicit that you cannot "reload" a group on the turn it is captured.

cards.name: Technomagi
cards.text: You have +4 on any attempt to control any Science, Magic, or Computer group. Your power structure is immune to attacks from Criminal groups. SPECIAL GOAL: Each Magic Artifact or Gadget Resource card in your power structure may count as a group toward the number of groups you need to control in order to win. No more than half the groups needed to win may be Resources.

cards.name: Tennis-Star
cards.text: Your Power Structure gets a +4 to defend against any attack involving a Media group. She gets a +10 against any attack involving a Media group. Female tennis star.

cards.name: Terrorist Nuke
cards.headline: Linked to 9/11
cards.context: Shows an explosion at a twin-tower skyscraper. Artist John Grigni said it reflected post-Cold War terrorism fears, not a prophecy — and noted a real tactical nuke would devastate far more than one building.

cards.name: The Day the Wall Came Down
The First Thing We Do, Let's Kill All The Lawyers
The Network
cards.text: You start your turn by drawing two Plot cards, rather than one. SPECIAL GOAL: Any Computer group with a power of 3 or more counts double toward your toal number of groups controlled.

cards.name: The OMNI
cards.text: You have +5 on any attempt to control any Church group. If you fail to control a Church group from your hand, you need not discard it - you may place it back anywhere in your Groups pile. SPECIAL GOAL: Control a total Power of 40 or more, counting The OMNI's Power. Up to 3 of your Church groups' Power values may be doubled for this Goal.

cards.name: The Oregon Crud
cards.text: Has a Power of 24, and destroys on a roll that succeeds by 10 or more.

cards.name: The Outer Church of the Archons
cards.text: Having a power of 0 does not prevent your groups from getting tokens. At any time, you may spend Archons token to put 1 token on one or more groups, as long as their total power does not exceed that of the Archons. SPECIAL GOALS: If any 8 groups you control have a combined power of 8 or less, you may claim victory. Paralyzation or devastation does not prevent your groups and their puppets from counting towards this goal.

cards.name: The Technocracy
cards.text: +4 on any attempt to destroy a Magic group. +2 on any attempt to control a Computer, Science, or Space group. This bonus is cumulative with each attribute. SPECIAL GOAL: Every two Gadget Resources you control count as one group towards the basic goal.

cards.name: The White Hand
cards.text: When any player makes a non-instant attack, you play assassinations to add their power, and the token they allow, to the attack. SPECIAL GOAL: The killing of up to 3 Personalities count as 2 Groups each towards the basic goal. If you can kill 5 Personalities without recourse to destroy the Moonbase, you may claim victory on that basis alone.

cards.name: The World Ends Tomorrow and You May Die!
Topmodell
cards.text: Gives +6 on any attempt to control any male Media Personality. By using her action, she can cancel any action of any Media group. Supermodel.

cards.name: U.S.A.
cards.text: German Version: USA In English-language tournaments: Not legal, because - according to local Illuminated propaganda - nobody can ever control the whole US at once, even its own government. From across the Atlantic it looks different . . .

cards.name: UFOs
cards.text: The UFOs have two actions per turn - they get two action tokens! These may not be used in the same attack. SPECIAL GOAL: The UFOs can have up to 3 different Goal cards in play, and win with any of them.

cards.name: Upheaval!
cards.text: Requires an Illuminati action, and cannot be used on your first turn.

cards.name: USA
cards.text: Gives +10 for direct control of the United Nation. Gives +4 on any attempt to destroy Violent groups. In English-language tournaments: Not legal, because - according to local Illuminated propaganda - nobody can ever control the whole US at once, even its own government. From across the Atlantic it looks different . . .

cards.name: Venus Williams
cards.text: German Version: Tennis-Star

cards.name: Video Games
cards.text: Gives +1 Power to all your other (i.e. not itself) Computer Groups.

cards.name: Volcano
cards.text: Has a Power of 18, and destroys on a roll that succeeds by 2 or more.

cards.name: Voodoo Economics
cards.text: Requires an Illuminati action, and can be used only once per game by each player.

cards.name: Weather Satellite
cards.text: Gives a +10 (not a +8), to Tornado, Hurricane, and Rain of Frogs (not Tidal Wave).

cards.name: You'd Pay to Know What You Really Think!
Zuvieldienstleistende
cards.text: Be careful, gramps! Your wheel might be loose! By using this group's action, you can decrease the Power of any one Conservative group by 3, or increase the Power of any one Liberal group by 3. This effect lasts until the beginning of your next turn. The "Zivieldienstleistende" are those who have chosen alternative service (in hospitals or social work) rather than be drafted into the military. Changing the first two letters to "Zu" rather than "Zi" is a German pun implying that they over-exert themselves . . . which is pure sarcasm.
